resources.string: How do I put my device in pairing mode?

This is the identifier written to the device with AT+WRMID.

• IDEAL_AP — the value the official IDEAL app always sends. It is hardcoded in the original app (it never sends anything else). Choose this to match the stock app exactly.

• IDEAL_AP30_PRO / AP40 / AP60 / AP80 / AP140_PRO — these are model identifiers taken from the official app's internal device list (the na.c enum). The stock app does NOT write these via AT+WRMID; they are offered here only as alternatives in case a device or the cloud expects a model-specific id.

If in doubt, pick IDEAL_AP.
IDEAL_AP is what the official app sends. The model names are extra options from the app\'s device list.

IDEAL Air Purifier (AP30 / AP40 / AP60 / AP80 / AP140 Pro)

1. Make sure the purifier is plugged in and powered on.
2. Press and hold the device's Wi-Fi / power button until the LED ring flashes PINK (magenta).
3. Pink flashing = pairing mode. The purifier is now broadcasting its own open Wi-Fi access point named "IDEAL…".
4. Keep your phone next to the device, then tap "Provision real device". Accept the system prompt to join the IDEAL network.

Official-app wording: "Follow the instruction until the light of the air purifier flashes pink, then press next."

IDEAL Air Sensor (AS10 / AS15)

1. Power on the sensor.
2. Touch and hold your hand on the top of the device until the light turns WHITE.
3. White light = pairing mode. The sensor is now broadcasting its open access point named "ideal-add-device…".
4. Keep your phone close, then tap "Provision real device".

Official-app wording: "Touch and hold your hand on top of the device until the light turns white, then press next."

(The official sensor screen shows no video — text instructions only.)
Put the device in pairing mode
